name: The Homeowners Employment Corporation
description_short: HUD-certified housing counseling agency offering foreclosure prevention, homebuyer education, and employment assistance to help homeowners retain their properties.
description_long:
The Homeowners Employment Corporation is a HUD-Approved Housing Counseling Agency and Mortgage Brokerage that specializes in helping homeowners avoid foreclosure and prospective buyers prepare for home purchase. Founded on the principle that employment stability is essential to mortgage sustainability, the organization combines housing counseling with employment assistance services. The company operates through both online workshops and telephone-based counseling by appointment, making services accessible to homeowners facing financial hardship. Their dual focus on housing and employment support reflects a unique understanding of the interconnected challenges homeowners face during economic difficulty.

The organization offers comprehensive homebuyer education programs, foreclosure prevention counseling, and mortgage brokerage services for loan applications. Their services include the eHome America Online Workshop for self-paced learning, instructor-led workshops for structured education, and individual counseling sessions focused on money management and credit improvement. For existing homeowners in distress, they provide loss mitigation options and resources to prevent foreclosure. Notably, they have partnered with Goodwill and local employment ministries to offer job search assistance, resume development, and employment search resources—a distinctive service tier that addresses the root cause of payment difficulties rather than treating symptoms.

What distinguishes this organization is the explicit integration of employment services with housing counseling. Rather than limiting support to mortgage and credit topics, they recognize that job loss or employment instability is a primary driver of foreclosure and incorporate employment assistance into their service model. Their HUD certification ensures compliance with federal housing counseling standards, and their partnerships with established employment organizations like Goodwill extend their credibility and reach. The availability of both group workshops and one-to-one counseling allows flexibility for different learning preferences and urgency levels.

As a non-profit HUD-certified agency, this company operates within the free-help category and does not charge for core counseling services. The primary limitation is that their mortgage brokerage function may create a potential conflict of interest—they both counsel homeowners on loan options and facilitate loan origination. Additionally, the website provides limited detail on response times, counselor qualifications, or specific loss mitigation success rates. Prospective clients should verify current service availability and any geographic restrictions before contacting them.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling agency, ensuring federal compliance and standardized service quality
Unique dual-service model combining housing counseling with employment assistance through Goodwill partnerships
Offers both group workshops (eHome America Online) and one-to-one counseling for flexible access
Foreclosure prevention focus includes specific loss mitigation resources for homeowners in hardship
Free-help services with no upfront charges for counseling, as non-profit agency
Homebuyer education includes practical credit-building and money management techniques
Multiple contact methods: phone counseling by appointment, online workshops, and in-person options
cons: Website lacks detail on counselor credentials, response times, or documented success rates for foreclosure prevention
Mortgage brokerage function creates potential conflict of interest between advising clients and originating loans
No clear information about geographic service area or whether services are available nationwide vs. regional only
Limited transparency on specific loss mitigation programs offered or approval rates
Website does not specify whether employment assistance services are available to all clients or in all locations
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage payment difficulty seeking loss mitigation options
First-time homebuyers wanting combined education on credit, money management, and the purchase process
Unemployed or underemployed homeowners who need both housing counseling and job search assistance
Borrowers preparing to apply for mortgages and wanting HUD-certified pre-purchase guidance
diagnosis: Best for homeowners in foreclosure risk or first-time buyers seeking comprehensive, free HUD-certified counseling that addresses both housing and employment stability. The main caveat is that their dual role as both counselor and mortgage broker warrants careful attention to ensure advice is unbiased, and clients should independently verify service availability in their location and the specific terms of any mortgage brokerage services.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling by phone appointment
eHome America Online Workshop for homebuyer education
One-to-one counseling for credit improvement and money management
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation guidance
Mortgage loan application processing and brokerage services
Employment search resources and job training assistance
Resume development support through Goodwill partnership
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1775 Parker Rd Se, Building C - Suite 210, Conyers, GA 30094
